Something wild is happening in AI right now. Two models just drop that. When you stack them together, work like an AI operating system. One thinks, one builds, one plans the whole thing out, the other just executes it fast. We're talking GLM5 Turbo and Google Gemini. And no, this isn't just another AI tool video. This is about wiring two completely different models together to run entire workflows from start to finish. Research, strategy, content, automation, all of it. I'm going to show you exactly how this works with real prompts and real examples. By the end of this video, you're going to see why this stack might be the most practical AI setup available right now. Most people are using these models separately. That's a mistake because when you combine them, the whole thing becomes something completely different. So, stick around because I'm going to walk you through the exact workflows we use with real prompts you can copy today. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. And Julian reads every single comment, so drop one below and let him know you're here. Let's get straight into it because this one is genuinely exciting. There are two AI models making serious noise right now. GLM5 Turbo built by GPU AI and Google Gemini, which most people are massively underusing. separately. They're both powerful, but when you combine them inside a single workflow, that's when things get really interesting. That's what this video is about. Not just what these models are, but how to actually use them together to save time, move faster, and get better outputs across everything you do. Let's start with GLM5 Turbo because most people outside of the AI research world haven't really heard of it yet, and that's going to change fast. GLM5 is built by Jepu AI. It's a next generation large language model with a massive architecture sitting around 745 billion parameters. Now that number sounds technical, but here's the part that actually matters for you. It uses something called mixture of experts architecture, which basically means the model doesn't activate its entire brain for every single task. It only fires up the parts it needs for what you're asking it to do. That makes it fast. Seriously fast. GLM5 Turbo is the speed optimized version of that built specifically for high-speed inference, AI agent workflows, automation pipelines, and tool orchestration. Think of it as the execution engine of your AI stack. It doesn't overthink. You give it a clear task, and it delivers coding, writing, building, automating. It handles all of it at a pace that makes it genuinely useful for real production workflows rather than just one-off chat prompts. If AI was a business, GLM5 Turbo would be your best operations manager. The person who takes a plan and just gets it done without needing to be handheld through every step. Now, Gemini is a completely different animal. And it's important to understand why. Gemini isn't trying to be the fastest executive. It's built to be the most connected and contextaware model available right now. And the big shift with Gemini recently is the depth of integration it has with Google Workspace. We're talking Docs, Sheets, Slides, Drive, and Gmail. You can literally describe what you want and Gemini builds it. A full spreadsheet, a presentation, a research summary, a full strategy document, and because it can pull context directly from your existing files and emails, everything it produces is more relevant and personalized to your actual situation. Gemini's real strength is multimodal reasoning. It can work with text, images, and audio. It can research deeply, plan thoroughly, summarize complex information, and turn all of that into something immediately actionable. Where GLM5 Turbo is your operations manager who executes the plan, Gemini is your strategist, your researcher, the one who maps everything out and figures out the right direction before anyone starts building. Two completely different strengths, two completely different roles. And that's exactly why the combination is so powerful. Here's the moment where the whole thing clicks. Layer one is Gemini. That's your thinking layer. research, planning, reasoning, context, and strategy all live here. Layer 2 is GLM5 Turbo. That's your execution layer. Agents, automation, building, writing, and producing all live here. Gemini thinks GLM5 Turbo executes. When you wire those two layers together inside a single structured workflow, you've built yourself an AI team that handles an entire process from the first idea all the way through to the finished output. No switching between tools randomly. No losing context halfway through. Just a clean, connected system where each model does what it's best at and hands off to the next layer at the right moment. That is the stack. And it's more powerful in practice than most people realize from just reading about it. No fluff, no theory, just practical systems that work. Link is in the description below. Let me show you exactly what this looks like with a real example. And because everything on this channel is about practical application, I'm going to use this stack to show you how we'd use it to grow a community like the AI profit boardroom. Step one, Gemini handles the research. You open Gemini and type, research the biggest frustrations people face when trying to implement AI into their daily business workflow. What are the main blockers? What do they wish existed? Gemini comes back with rich, detailed audience intelligence, time confusion, tool overwhelm, not knowing where to start, information overload, not seeing results fast enough. That's your entire content and messaging strategy sitting right there. Step two, Gemini builds the strategy. You follow up with based on these frustrations, create a full positioning and content strategy for an AI automation community called the AI profit boardroom. The goal is to position it as the clearest, most practical resource for entrepreneurs who want to use AI to save time and work smarter. Gemini returns a complete strategy document, messaging framework, audience targeting, content pillars, and positioning angles. Done. Step three, GLM5 Turbo takes over and executes. You hand it that strategy and say, "Using this strategy, write a compelling landing page for the AI profit boardroom. Keep it punchy, benefit driven, and simple. Help busy entrepreneurs understand exactly what they get and why joining makes sense for them right now. GLM5 Turbo produces the full page copy fast. Step four, GLM5 Turbo keeps going without stopping. It generates the full onboarding email sequence for new members. It writes the YouTube scripts, LinkedIn posts, short form content, and ad copy, all from the same strategic foundation that Gemini built in step two. The consistency across every piece of content is automatic because it's all rooted in the same strategy. Step five, you bring Gemini back in to analyze everything that was produced. It identifies what's strong, what needs sharpening, and gives you a specific improvement plan. Then GLM5 Turbo implements every change. That full loop research, strategy, execution, analysis, improvement is a complete AI powered growth system running inside one connected workflow. Let's run another one quickly because this applies to anyone building a channel, a newsletter, or any kind of content business. Gemini step. What are the most searched and underserved questions people are asking right now about using AI for productivity and business automation? Gemini pulls current trends and content gaps from across the web. You now have a full content road map built on real audience demand rather than guesswork. GLM5 Turboep. Write 10 YouTube video scripts based on these topics. Keep each one under 1500 words. Punchy, conversational, and valuep packed with no filler. Done in minutes. That is a full month of content produced in under an hour. When you see it happen the first time, it genuinely changes how you think about content production. Two things before you go.
